alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices and Services building and scaling global commercial operations with cross-functional stakeholder management and partnership ownership
alignments.job_requirement: Build and nurture long-term relationships with key clients, serving as their primary point of contact for technical matters

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product Operations and Commercial Operations leadership experience identifying optimization and scaling opportunities across global operations and transformation programs
alignments.job_requirement: Understand clients' business needs and goals, proactively identify opportunities to optimize their payment solutions

alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years of strategy and operations experience with strong analytical capabilities: SQL/BigQuery, advanced Excel/Sheets, Looker/Tableau dashboarding; manages stakeholders across technical and commercial teams
alignments.job_requirement: 3 years of experience in an analytical or technical role with experience managing internal and external stakeholders

alignments.candidate_evidence: Strategy and Operations leadership background with demonstrated cross-functional communication and training/enablement at Google scale
alignments.job_requirement: Effective presentation skills and ability to engage with both technical and non-technical stakeholders

gaps.gap: No Customer Success, account management, or dedicated TAM experience; background is strategy and operations, not quota-carrying CS or client-facing support
gaps.mitigation: Commercial operations and stakeholder management translate partially to account relationships, but pure CS/TAM domain knowledge and rhythms would require ramp-up

gaps.gap: No demonstrated payments or fintech domain experience; Checkout.com role assumes deep knowledge of payment methods, 3DS/SCA/AVS/MIT, PCI-DSS/PSD2, and API-based payment integrations
gaps.mitigation: Candidate has strong analytical foundations and process-design capability to learn payments domain, but domain depth is material gap for a TAM role

gaps.gap: Limited hands-on technical depth in front-end/back-end technologies (JavaScript, CSS, HTML) required to debug integrations and provide technical guidance on payment flows
gaps.mitigation: Basic Python and SQL proficiency exist, but deeper front-end/API troubleshooting experience is not evident; would require domain-specific upskilling

gaps.gap: No crypto or iGaming vertical expertise called out in title and role context
gaps.mitigation: Operational principles transfer, but vertical-specific regulatory and technical nuances (crypto AML, gaming licensing, chargebacks) require onboarding

output.level_rationale: Scope centers on client relationship ownership, technical troubleshooting, project coordination, and product feedback—typical L4 (Senior Manager / Senior IC) specialist / account role. Company stage (10B daily transactions, 20 global offices) and explicit 3-year requirement support L4. No manager-of-managers or VP-level scope indicated.
output.summary: Technical Account Management role for Checkout.com's Crypto & iGaming vertical. Candidate brings strong operations and stakeholder-management experience but lacks Customer Success, payments-domain, and dedicated fintech/TAM background. Role requires deep knowledge of payment flows, compliance frameworks (3DS, PCI-DSS, PSD2), API integrations, and vertical-specific regulations (crypto AML, gaming chargeback rules). This is a deprioritized function family (CS/account management without strategic transformation scope) for the candidate's target profile, and domain and function mismatch is material despite strong analytical and process-design foundations.
uncertainties: Company size and stage not explicit in role JSON; inferred from description (10B transactions, 20 offices) but formal org context missing
Reporting structure and broader scope of 'Senior Specialist' title not specified—could indicate individual contributor or light team scope
Crypto and iGaming vertical-specific requirements and regulatory complexity not detailed; assumed material based on title
Degree requirement stated as 'desirable' rather than 'required,' supporting equivalent practical experience equivalence
